key_insight: The paper turns a nominal trajectory into a robust one by learning a structured affine correction policy that enforces chance constraints using sampled uncertainty, without assuming a known noise distribution.
engineering_relevance: This offers a transferable template for spacecraft and aerospace trajectory planning where safety, fuel efficiency, and uncertainty handling must be balanced under limited probabilistic modeling.
method: Compute a deterministic nominal trajectory offline.
Use reinforcement learning to learn feedforward and time-varying feedback corrections.
Enforce probabilistic feasibility with rollout-based quantile constraints and terminal covariance penalties.
evidence_signal: The framework is evaluated on two distinct problems—3D Earth-Mars multi-impulse transfer and stochastic atmospheric rocket landing—and is tested beyond training conditions, including Gaussian, bounded uniform, and unseen process disturbances.
watch_item: Performance depends on sampled rollouts and may be sensitive to quantile tuning, covariance penalties, and coverage of the uncertainty set used during training.
paper_title: Distribution-Agnostic Robust Trajectory Optimization via Chance-Constrained Reinforcement Learning
